rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 23, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT BRANNAN STREET FITNESS, INC. DBA GOLD'S MOTION To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution. Granted, no opposition filed. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 23, 2011, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendant's demurrer is overruled. The first amended cross-complaint pleads the legal intendment and effect of the agreement, which is sufficient. Construction Protective Services v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 198-99. All other arguments are factual issues not properly decided on demurrer. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: KIM BELLINO VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To 1) Compel Responses To Interrogatories; 2) To Compel Further Respones To Production Of Documents, And 3) For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 23, 2011, line 1. DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC., LENARD RITZMAN'S Motion To 1) Compel Responses To Interrogatories; 2) To Compel Further Respones To Production Of Documents, And 3) For Sanctions. Defendants motion to compel response to interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff's counsel must remit $2000 in sanctions and responses to defendant's counsel by September 9, 2011. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PAYNE & FEARS, LLP VS. KATHLEEN SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sactions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 23, 2011, line 5. PLAINTIFF PAYNE & FEARS, LLP Motion For Terminating Sactions. Plaintiff's motion for terminating sanctions is granted. Defendant has twice failed to sit for deposition despite a subpoena and the written and oral orders of this court. Moreover, plaintiff's excuse that she won't come to California because there is a bench warrant out for her arrest (which was not told to this court previously) does not provide an adequate excuse to avoid another order of this court. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN WALSH VS. NORTH BEACH CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 23, 2011, line 7. DEFENDANT NORTH BEACH CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's complaint is barred under the doctrines of Res Judicata and Collateral Estoppel. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: HONGIK KIM VS. JINSIK IN et al

Code 5710 and 5056. Defendants must bring a separate motion to require plaintiff to post a bond. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: 350 BEACH LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 23, 2011, line 10. PETITIONER 350 BEACH LLC Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus. Petitioner's motion for a writ of administrative mandamus is denied. The court finds that the Board of Appeal did not abuse its discretion under the substantial evidence list. The Board relied on substantial evidence when they upheld the Zoning Administrator's decision. =(302/LMG)
